segments.text: 250,000 sea nails crushed so that this one man could wear Tyrion purple.
It starts clear, then sunlight turns it into yellow, green, blue, and finally a shade of purple known as Tyrion purple.
One drop personnel. To fill a diapot,
you need mountains of shell. Tyrion purple wasn't fashion. It was power. And only the richest like Julius Caesar can wear it. In Rome, wearing this shade without a rank could get you punished.
Some rulers even made this a death level crime. The color barely faded and the dye smelled like sea. That's how a color became a symbol of status. For more amazing design stories, subscribe.
